sources.notes: Interactive problem: query range sums of a hidden generated array and find its maximum value.
Good Bye 2025 editorial hints use sum invariance, final split, and recursion.
Detailed writeup: total sum is invariant under flatten and doubled by concatenate; binary search the equal-sum split and recurse into the shorter half.

annotation.summary: Interactively find the maximum value in an array generated by repeated flatten and concatenate operations using range-sum queries.
annotation.constraints: At most 300 queries are allowed, so the search must shrink the interval logarithmically.
annotation.core_idea: The hidden interval sum is always a power of two. A final operation induces a split into two equal-sum halves; binary search the split point with prefix-sum queries. The maximum lies in the shorter half because that side underwent fewer flatten operations. Recurse on that half until one element remains.

solution_variants.name: interactive-equal-sum-recursion
solution_variants.summary: Use range-sum binary search to split the current interval into equal-sum halves, then recurse into the shorter half.

tags.evidence: The solution is an adaptive range-sum query strategy.
Each recursion level binary searches the equal-sum split point.
The interval is recursively reduced to one half after finding a split.
The key observation is the invariant behavior of total sums under flatten/concatenate.
tags.description: Use a preserved quantity or structural invariant as the core of a solution.
tags.created_reason: Invariant-based reasoning is a broad but useful retrieval category distinct from implementation tags.
